author: m.kalinin
content: we can introduce another queue for rate limiting purposes, and the problem of a number of deposits in a block still exists, we can either extend the max_deposit_requests_per_payload or set a hard cap at 8,192 on the EL. The hard cap is a solution that will work for any further gas limit increase, so probably is preferrable one

author: m.kalinin
content: <@755590043632140352> why do we need the sig to be valid for a pending validator, re: https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/4897 ? Why simply pending validator with that same pubkey exists check isn't enough to address the original issue? Is there anything else addressed by requiring sig to be valid?

author: jih2nn
content: He answered it [here](https://discord.com/channels/595666850260713488/1468325521061318827/1468650725981687982) by the way.

author: nero_eth
content:
Since 512 has been proven to work for CLs, and, empirically, we see NOs don't really need more per block, I'd support that.
The introduced knapsack problem for block building can be mostly ignored as each valid deposit comes with at least 1 eth deposit which is an already strong anti-dos mechanism.

author: m.kalinin
content: 512 signature per block doesn't sound good for CLs 😄 but that's orthogonal, the hard cap will cut the dependency between MAX_DEPOSIT_REQUESTS_PER_PAYLOAD and gas limit which is good

author: m.kalinin
content: if CL will have an own builder sig verification rate limiting mechanism, e.g. a queue, then I would ask why not use 1,024 instead of 512?
